strategy: Kill order is the strategy: focus the Fire head FIRST. It never shields, traps placed on it are cheat-free, and once it dies the Balance and Myth heads can no longer cheat-cast their Tower Shields at all.

While all three heads are up, never attack a shielded head. Every 5 rounds the Balance and Myth heads each get three -40% Tower Shields; hitting a shielded head makes BOTH other heads retaliate for ~750 each — and multi-target spells trigger it twice.
Supports: put all traps on the Fire head only. The Balance and Myth heads strip traps cast on them unless you enchant with Indemnity (or Vaporize their cleanse).
Hitter: build Myth, Life, or Death damage (+30% boost on the Balance head; never Balance into 80% resist). Blades funnel to the hitter as usual.
Once Fire is down, trap the remaining heads freely (Indemnity still helps), and clear Balance and Myth with boosted AoE hits. Both are stunable — stuns cover your setup rounds.
If you must hit through shields, dispel first — dispels stop the retaliation cheat.
cheatNotes: On a 5-round timer, the Balance and Myth heads each stack three -40% Tower Shields on themselves. Plan your burst for the rounds before the refresh.
Hitting a head that still has its cheat shields makes the other two heads counter for roughly 750 damage each. With three heads up that's ~1,500 per mistake.
Dispelling a head prevents its retaliation hit — useful if you have to swing into shields.
Shadow creature spells count as attacks and set off the retaliation, so time them for shield-free heads.
AoE spells and Beguile trigger the retaliation twice. Save your AoE for after the Fire head is dead or shields are gone.
Call of Khrulhu is the exception — it doesn't set off the retaliation.
The Balance and Myth heads remove any trap you place on them. Put traps on the Fire head instead.
Vaporize dispels their trap-removal cheat if you need traps on Balance or Myth.
With Elemental or Spirit Trap, only the last trap of the three gets removed — the first two stick.
Pet may-cast traps don't trigger the removal.
Windstorm-type multi-traps don't trigger the removal either.
Indemnity on any trap spell fully protects it from being removed. Enchant your Feints.
The Fire head never casts Tower Shields — it's always safe to attack.
Shields sometimes trade between heads, so re-check who is shielded each round before committing a hit.
Traps on the Fire head are completely cheat-free. Stack everything there first.
Killing the Fire head shuts off the Tower Shield cheat for the other two heads permanently. This is why Fire dies first.
